channel.title: IT-Security & Privacy News Aggregator
channel.description: Aggregierter RSS-Feed aus führenden IT-Security, Privacy und Cyber Policy Quellen

item.title: [Virtual Event] Anatomy of a Data Breach: What to Do if it Happens to You
Infosecurity Europe
DocketWise Data Breach Impacts 143,000
item.description: <p>Hackers accessed names, addresses, Social Security numbers, financial information, and medical data from third-party partner repositories.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.securityweek.com/docketwise-data-breach-impacts-143000/">DocketWise Data Breach Impacts 143,000</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.securityweek.com">SecurityWeek</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Ionut Arghire

item.title: FBI Warns 'Kali365' Phishing Kit Hijacks Microsoft 365 OAuth Tokens
item.description: The Kali365 phishing-as-a-service platform lowers the barrier of entry for cybercriminals, said the FBI

item.title: Fake Streams, Counterfeit Merch and Other Scams: How Fraudsters Target F1 Fans
item.description: From fake F1 streams to counterfeit merch, fraudsters are exploiting fans online and the Bitdefender Cybersecurity Grand Prix Fan Threat Index details how

item.title: Over 5,500 GitHub Repositories Infected in ‘Megalodon’ Supply Chain Attack
item.description: <p>Fake automated commits injected GitHub Actions workflows containing payloads to steal credentials, CI secrets, keys, and tokens.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.securityweek.com/over-5500-github-repositories-infected-in-megalodon-supply-chain-attack/">Over 5,500 GitHub Repositories Infected in &#8216;Megalodon&#8217; Supply Chain Attack</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.securityweek.com">SecurityWeek</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Ionut Arghire

item.title: TrapDoor Supply Chain Attack Spreads Credential-Stealing Malware via npm, PyPI, and CratesIO
item.description:
A new coordinated cross-ecosystem software supply chain attack campaign has targeted npm, PyPI, and Crates.io to distribute credential-stealing malware.
The campaign, codenamed TrapDoor, spans more than 34 malicious packages across over 384 versions. The earliest activity was recorded on May 22, 2026, at 8:20 p.m. UTC, with new packages published to the ecosystems in waves from a cluster of
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: npm Adds 2FA-Gated Publishing and Package Install Controls Against Supply Chain Attacks
item.description:
GitHub has rolled out new controls for npm to improve the security of the software supply chain, giving maintainers the ability to explicitly approve a release prior to the packages becoming publicly available for installation.
Called staged publishing, the feature is now generally available on npm. It mandates that a human maintainer pass a two-factor authentication (2FA) challenge to approve
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Packagist Supply Chain Attack Infects 8 Packages Using GitHub-Hosted Linux Malware
item.description:
A new "coordinated" supply chain attack campaign has impacted eight packages on Packagist including malicious code designed to run a Linux binary retrieved from a GitHub Releases URL.
"Although the affected packages were all Composer packages, the malicious code was not added to composer.json," Socket said. "Instead, it was inserted into package.json, targeting projects that ship JavaScript
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Claude Mythos AI Finds 10,000 High-Severity Flaws in Widely Used Software
item.description:
Anthropic on Friday disclosed that Project Glasswing has helped uncover more than 10,000 high- or critical-severity vulnerabilities across some of the most "systemically" important software across the world since the cybersecurity initiative went live last month.
Project Glasswing is a defensive effort launched by the artificial intelligence (AI) company to secure critical global software
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: ‘Underminr’ Vulnerability Lets Attackers Hide Malicious Connections Behind Trusted Domains
item.description: <p>The stealthy vulnerability impacts roughly 88 million domains and can be exploited to bypass DNS filtering and hide command-and-control traffic.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.securityweek.com/underminr-vulnerability-lets-attackers-hide-malicious-connections-behind-trusted-domains/">‘Underminr’ Vulnerability Lets Attackers Hide Malicious Connections Behind Trusted Domains</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.securityweek.com">SecurityWeek</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Ionut Arghire

item.title: Laravel-Lang PHP Packages Compromised to Deliver Cross-Platform Credential Stealer
item.description:
Cybersecurity researchers have flagged a fresh software supply chain attack campaign that has targeted multiple PHP packages belonging to Laravel-Lang to deliver a comprehensive credential-stealing framework.
The affected packages include -
laravel-lang/lang laravel-lang/http-statuses laravel-lang/attributes laravel-lang/actions
"The timing and pattern of the newly published tags
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: LiteSpeed cPanel Plugin CVE-2026-48172 Exploited to Run Scripts as Root
item.description:
A maximum-severity security vulnerability impacting LiteSpeed User-End cPanel Plugin has come under active exploitation in the wild.
The flaw, tracked as CVE-2026-48172 (CVSS score: 10.0), relates to an instance of incorrect privilege assignment that an attacker could abuse to run arbitrary scripts with elevated permissions.
"Any cPanel user (including an attacker or a compromised account) may
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Drupal Core SQL Injection Bug Actively Exploited, Added to CISA KEV
item.description:
The U.S. Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) has added a recently patched critical security flaw impacting Drupal Core to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities (KEV) catalog, based on evidence of active exploitation.
The vulnerability in question is CVE-2026-9082 (CVSS score: 6.5), an SQL injection vulnerability affecting all supported versions of Drupal Core.
"Drupal Core
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Friday Squid Blogging: Regulating Squid Fishing in the South Pacific
item.dc:creator: Bruce Schneier

item.title: First VPN Dismantled in Global Takedown Over Use by 25 Ransomware Groups
item.description:
Authorities in Europe and North America have announced the dismantling of a criminal virtual private network (VPN) service used by criminal actors to obscure the origins of ransomware attacks, data theft, scanning, and denial-of-service attacks.
Codenamed Operation Saffron, the disruption of First VPN Service was led by France and the Netherlands, with several other nations supporting the
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Drupal Vulnerability in Hacker Crosshairs Shortly After Disclosure
item.description: <p>Drupal is warning users that it has already seen attempts to exploit CVE-2026-9082 and security firms are seeing attacks against thousands of websites.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.securityweek.com/drupal-vulnerability-in-hacker-crosshairs-shortly-after-disclosure/">Drupal Vulnerability in Hacker Crosshairs Shortly After Disclosure</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.securityweek.com">SecurityWeek</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Eduard Kovacs

item.title: Lawmakers Demand Answers as CISA Tries to Contain Data Leak
item.description: Lawmakers in both houses of Congress are demanding answers from the U.S. Cybersecurity &#038; Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) after KrebsOnSecurity reported this week that a CISA contractor intentionally published AWS GovCloud keys and a vast trove of other agency secrets on a public GitHub account. The inquiry comes as CISA is still struggling to contain the breach and invalidate the leaked credentials.
item.dc:creator: BrianKrebs

item.title: Ghostwriter Targets Ukraine Government Entities with Prometheus Phishing Malware
item.description:
The Belarus-aligned threat actor known as Ghostwriter (aka UAC-0057 and UNC1151) has been observed using lures related to Prometheus, a Ukrainian online learning platform, to target government organizations in the country.
The activity, per the Computer Emergency Response Team of Ukraine (CERT-UA), involves sending phishing emails to government entities using compromised accounts. It's been
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Akamai Joins Growing Chorus of Vendors Betting Big on Secure Enterprise Browsers
item.description: When Akamai announced its LayerX acquisition, the company joined a growing list of vendors adding secure enterprise browsers to their product portfolios.
item.dc:creator: Jeffrey Schwartz

item.title: Microsoft Warns: Windows Zero-Day ‘YellowKey’ Can Bypass BitLocker
item.description: <p>Microsoft has released a temporary mitigation for YellowKey, a Windows zero-day that can reportedly bypass BitLocker protections.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com/article/news-microsoft-yellowkey-bitlocker-bypass-mitigation/">Microsoft Warns: Windows Zero-Day ‘YellowKey’ Can Bypass BitLocker</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com">TechRepublic</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Joseph Ofonagoro

item.title: In Other News: Industrial Router Exploitation, CISA KEV Nomination Form, Gas Station Hacking
item.description: <p>Other noteworthy stories that might have slipped under the radar: CISA contractor exposes credentials, Mythos testing and new features, Huawei router flaw triggered telecom blackout.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.securityweek.com/in-other-news-industrial-router-exploitation-cisa-kev-nomination-form-gas-station-hacking/">In Other News: Industrial Router Exploitation, CISA KEV Nomination Form, Gas Station Hacking</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.securityweek.com">SecurityWeek</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: SecurityWeek News

item.title: AI Overhauls, IPOs, and Cyberthreats Define This Week in Tech
item.description: <p>See what you missed in Daily Tech Insider from May 18–22.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com/article/ai-overhauls-ipos-and-cyberthreats-define-this-week-in-tech/">AI Overhauls, IPOs, and Cyberthreats Define This Week in Tech</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com">TechRepublic</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: TechRepublic Staff

item.title: CISA Security Leak
item.dc:creator: Bruce Schneier

item.title: Verizon DBIR: Healthcare Fends Off Increased Social Engineering Attacks
item.description: Ransomware and vendor breaches persist. The &quot;2026 Data Breach Investigations Report&quot; (DBIR) highlights how evolving social engineering tactics make the sector more vulnerable.
item.dc:creator: Arielle Waldman

item.title: Canadian Man Arrested for Operating Kimwolf Botnet
item.description: <p>Jacob Butler, 23, has been arrested in Canada and US authorities are seeking his extradition on computer hacking charges.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.securityweek.com/canadian-man-arrested-for-operating-kimwolf-botnet/">Canadian Man Arrested for Operating Kimwolf Botnet</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.securityweek.com">SecurityWeek</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Eduard Kovacs

item.title: Megalodon GitHub Attack Targets 5,561 Repos with Malicious CI/CD Workflows
item.description:
Cybersecurity researchers have disclosed details of a new automated campaign called Megalodon that has pushed 5,718 malicious commits to 5,561 GitHub repositories within a six-hour window.
"Using throwaway accounts and forged author identities (build-bot, auto-ci, ci-bot, pipeline-bot), the attacker injected GitHub Actions workflows containing base64-encoded bash payloads that exfiltrate CI
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Making Vulnerable Drivers Exploitable Without Hardware - The BYOVD Perspective
item.description:
1 Introduction
This article provides a technical analysis of how many Windows kernel mode drivers can be interacted with from user mode without the hardware they were developed for. This work was motivated by driver-oriented vulnerability research and the need to evaluate the exploitability of individual findings, which frequently affect code whose reachability is hardware-gated. The
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Fake Gemini and Claude Code Sites Spread Infostealers Through SEO Poisoning
item.description: The infostealer payload in this campaign collect a vast amount of data, from collaboration authentication keys to cryptocurrency wallets

item.title: Apple Blocked $2.2bn in App Store Fraud in the Last Year
item.description: Total figure for fraudulent transactions Apple has blocked since 2020 now stands at over $11bn

item.title: ‘First VPN’ Cybercrime Service Disrupted, Administrator Arrested
item.description: <p>The FBI says First VPN has been used by dozens of ransomware groups for network reconnaissance and intrusions.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.securityweek.com/first-vpn-cybercrime-service-disrupted-administrator-arrested/">&#8216;First VPN&#8217; Cybercrime Service Disrupted, Administrator Arrested</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.securityweek.com">SecurityWeek</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Eduard Kovacs

item.title: Kimwolf DDoS Botnet Operator Arrested in Canada Over DDoS-for-Hire Attacks
item.description:
The U.S. Department of Justice (DoJ) on Thursday announced the arrest of a Canadian man in connection with allegedly operating a distributed denial-of-service (DDoS) botnet known as Kimwolf.
In tandem, Jacob Butler (aka Dort), 23, Ottawa, Canada, has been charged with offenses related to the development and operation of the botnet. Kimwolf is assessed to be a variant of AISURU that specifically
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Foul play: Fake FIFA websites target soccer fans looking for World Cup tickets, merchandise
item.description: Watch out for bogus World Cup websites that mimic official ticket and merchandise flows to steal money and personal data

item.title: TrendAI Patches Apex One Zero-Day Exploited in the Wild
item.description: <p>CVE-2026-34926 is a directory traversal flaw that can be exploited against the on-premise version of Apex One.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.securityweek.com/trendai-patches-apex-one-zero-day-exploited-in-the-wild/">TrendAI Patches Apex One Zero-Day Exploited in the Wild</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.securityweek.com">SecurityWeek</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Eduard Kovacs

item.title: Grafana Says Codebase and Other Data Stolen via TanStack Supply Chain Attack
item.description: <p>Hackers accessed Grafana’s GitHub repositories after a token compromised in the TanStack attack was not rotated.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.securityweek.com/grafana-says-codebase-and-other-data-stolen-via-tanstack-supply-chain-attack/">Grafana Says Codebase and Other Data Stolen via TanStack Supply Chain Attack</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.securityweek.com">SecurityWeek</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Ionut Arghire

item.title: China's Webworm Uses Discord, Microsoft Graphs to Hack EU Governments
item.description: The advanced persistent threat group also relied on SOCKS proxies like SoftEther VPN, tunneling tools that act as a middleman between victim and attacker.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Culafi

item.title: CISA Adds Exploited Langflow and Trend Micro Apex One Vulnerabilities to KEV
item.description:
The U.S. Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) on Thursday added two security flaws impacting Langflow and Trend Micro Apex One to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities (KEV) catalog, citing evidence of active exploitation.
The vulnerabilities in question are listed below -
CVE-2025-34291 (CVSS score: 9.4) - An origin validation error vulnerability in Langflow that could
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Cisco Patches CVSS 10.0 Secure Workload REST API Flaw Enabling Data Access
item.description:
Cisco has rolled out updates for a maximum-severity security flaw impacting Secure Workload that could allow an unauthenticated, remote attacker to access sensitive data.
Tracked as CVE-2026-20223 (CVSS score: 10.0), the vulnerability arises from insufficient validation and authentication when accessing REST API endpoints.
"An attacker could exploit this vulnerability if they are able to send
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Alleged Kimwolf Botmaster ‘Dort’ Arrested, Charged in U.S. and Canada
item.description: Canadian authorities on Wednesday arrested a 23-year-old Ottawa man on suspicion of building and operating Kimwolf, a fast spreading Internet-of-Things botnet that enslaved millions of devices for use in a series of massive distributed denial-of-service (DDoS) attacks over the past six months. KrebsOnSecurity publicly named the suspect in February 2026 after the accused launched a volley of DDoS, doxing and swatting campaigns against this author and a security researcher. He now faces criminal hacking charges in both Canada and the United States.
item.dc:creator: BrianKrebs

item.title: Google API Keys Remain Active After Deletion
item.description: A security researcher discovered the API keys can still be used for up to 23 minutes after deletion, even though the cloud provider claims deletion is immediate.
item.dc:creator: Rob Wright

item.title: New Verizon Report Reveals the Security Gap Attackers Are Exploiting Most
item.description: <p>Verizon’s 2026 DBIR shows vulnerability exploitation, AI-enabled attacks, third-party risk, and ransomware are reshaping cyber threats.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com/article/news-verizon-dbir-vulnerability-exploitation-2026/">New Verizon Report Reveals the Security Gap Attackers Are Exploiting Most</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com">TechRepublic</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Ken Underhill

item.title: Proofpoint Integrates with the Claude Compliance API to Extend Data Security and Governance to Claude
macOS Kernel Memory Corruption Exploit
item.description: <p>A group used Anthropic&#8217;s Mythos AI model to <a href="https://blog.calif.io/p/first-public-kernel-memory-corruption">help find</a> a kernel memory corruption vulnerability and exploit on Apple&#8217;s M5.</p> <p>News <a href="https://9to5mac.com/2026/05/14/calif-team-details-how-anthropic-mythos-helped-build-a-working-macos-exploit-in-five-days/">article</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Bruce Schneier

item.title: AI Agents Are Shifting Identity Security Budget Dynamics
item.description: AI agent projects are proliferating throughout the enterprise, and those AI agent identities require management, security, and governance. New Omdia research shows the AI agent identity budget dynamics are very different than traditional IAM projects.
item.dc:creator: Todd Thiemann

item.title: Cybercriminal VPN Dismantled in Europol Crackdown
item.description: First VPN, a service used by ransomware actors and fraudsters, was dismantled by Europol

item.title: GitHub Breach Traced to Malicious 'Nx Console' VS Code Extension
item.description: A threat actor compromised an Nx developer and posed as a legitimate maintainer to publish a malicious extension on Visual Studio Marketplace

item.title: Showboat Linux Malware Hits Middle East Telecom with SOCKS5 Proxy Backdoor
item.description:
Cybersecurity researchers have disclosed details of a new Linux malware dubbed Showboat that has been put to use in a campaign targeting a telecommunications provider in the Middle East since at least mid-2022.
"Showboat is a modular post-exploitation framework designed for Linux systems, capable of spawning a remote shell, transferring files, and functioning as a SOCKS5 proxy," Lumen
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Chinese APTs Share Linux Backdoor in Central Asia Telco Attacks
item.description: &quot;Showboat&quot; doesn't show off, but clearly it doesn't need to, as it's long helped China spy on small market communications providers.
item.dc:creator: Nate Nelson

item.title: Content Delivery Exploit Opens Websites to Brand Hijacking
item.description: The Underminr domain-fronting attack allows threat actors to modify Web requests and leverage trusted websites to cloak malicious activity.
item.dc:creator: Nate Nelson

item.title: Three-Quarters of Firms Knowingly Ship Vulnerable Code
item.description: AI risks threaten to permeate supply chains through unvetted code and unaudited suppliers

item.title: Cisco Patches Critical Vulnerability in Secure Workload
item.description: <p>Insufficient validation and authentication in the Secure Workload’s REST APIs provide remote attackers with Site Admin privileges.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.securityweek.com/cisco-patches-critical-vulnerability-in-secure-workload/">Cisco Patches Critical Vulnerability in Secure Workload</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.securityweek.com">SecurityWeek</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Ionut Arghire

item.title: Nine-Year-Old Linux Kernel Flaw Leaks SSH Keys and Password Hashes
item.description: Qualys finds nine-year-old Linux ptrace flaw exposing SSH keys and password hashes locally

item.title: ThreatsDay Bulletin: Linux Rootkits, Router 0-Day, AI Intrusions, Scam Kits and 25 New Stories
item.description:
This week starts small.
A token leaks. A bad package slips in. A login trick works. An old tool shows up again. At first, it feels like the usual mess. Then you see the pattern: attackers are not always breaking in. They are using the parts we already trust.
That is what makes it worrying. The danger is in normal things now - updates, apps, cloud buttons, support chats, trusted accounts. AI
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Microsoft Warns of Two Actively Exploited Defender Vulnerabilities
item.description:
Microsoft has disclosed that a privilege escalation and a denial-of-service flaw in Defender has come under active exploitation in the wild.
The former, tracked as CVE-2026-41091, is rated 7.8 on the CVSS scoring system. Successful exploitation of the flaw could allow an attacker to gain SYSTEM privileges.
"Improper link resolution before file access ('link following') in Microsoft Defender
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: When Identity is the Attack Path
item.description: Consider a cached access key on a single Windows machine. It got there the way most cached credentials do - a user logged in, and the key stored itself automatically. Standard AWS behavior. No one misconfigured anything or violated a policy. Yet that single key, which was easily accessible to a minor-league attacker, could have opened a path to some 98% of entities in the company's cloud
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Grafana Labs Says Code Breach Stemmed from TanStack Attack
item.description: Grafana Labs has confirmed a recent data breach was caused by the TanStack supply chain attack

item.title: 9-Year-Old Linux Kernel Flaw Enables Root Command Execution on Major Distros
item.description:
Cybersecurity researchers have disclosed details of a vulnerability in the Linux kernel that remained undetected for nine years.
The vulnerability, tracked as CVE-2026-46333 (CVSS score: 5.5), is a case of improper privilege management that could permit an unprivileged local user to disclose sensitive files and execute arbitrary commands as root on default installations of several major
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: GitHub Internal Repositories Breached via Malicious Nx Console VS Code Extension
item.description:
GitHub on Wednesday officially confirmed that the breach of its internal repositories was the result of a compromise of an employee device involving a poisoned version of the Nx Console Microsoft Visual Studio Code (VS Code) extension.&nbsp;
The development comes as the Nx team revealed that the extension, nrwl.angular-console, was breached after one of its developers' systems was hacked in the
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Highly Critical Drupal Core Flaw Exposes PostgreSQL Sites to RCE Attacks
item.description: Drupal has released security updates for a "highly critical" security vulnerability in Drupal Core that could be exploited by attackers to achieve remote code execution, privilege escalation, or information disclosure. The vulnerability, now tracked as CVE-2026-9082, carries a CVSS score of 6.5 out of 10.0, per CVE.org. Drupal said the vulnerability resides in a database abstraction API that is
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Microsoft Disrupts Malware-Signing Service Used by Ransomware Gangs
item.description: <p>Microsoft disrupted Fox Tempest, a malware-signing service accused of abusing Azure certificates to disguise ransomware and malware as trusted software.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com/article/news-microsoft-fox-tempest-malware-signing-service/">Microsoft Disrupts Malware-Signing Service Used by Ransomware Gangs</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com">TechRepublic</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Ken Underhill

item.title: Cyber Pros Can't Decide If AI Is a Good or a Bad Thing
item.description: There is nothing cybersecurity professionals are more excited about, and nothing they fear more, than AI.
item.dc:creator: Nate Nelson

item.title: GitHub Confirms Breach, 4K Internal Repos Stolen
item.description: GitHub confirmed a data breach this week involving the theft of thousands of developer code repositories. One threat actor — TeamPCP — took credit.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Culafi

item.title: Fake Android Apps Commit Carrier Billing Fraud for Premium Services
item.description: The disguised apps use WebView automation, JavaScript injection, and OTP interception to avoid detection and complete fraudulent subscriptions.
item.dc:creator: Jai Vijayan

item.title: Processes &amp; Culture Top Reasons Behind Data Breaches
item.description: In spite of state laws meant to improve cyber hygiene, an analysis of incidents shows issues persist and visibility falls short.
item.dc:creator: Arielle Waldman

item.title: Microsoft Open-Sources RAMPART and Clarity to Secure AI Agents During Development
item.description:
Microsoft has unveiled two new open-source tools called RAMPART and Clarity to assist developers in better testing the security of artificial intelligence (AI) agents.
RAMPART, short for Risk Assessment and Measurement Platform for Agentic Red Teaming, functions as a Pytest-native safety and security testing framework for writing and running safety and security tests for AI agents, covering
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Patch Now: Critical Flaw in OT Robot OS Gives Attackers Control
item.description: An unauthenticated attacker can exploit the command injection vulnerability to gain remote access to robotic systems, causing significant disruption to the environment.
item.dc:creator: Elizabeth Montalbano

item.title: Android Malware Campaign Used Hundreds of Fake Apps to Silently Charge Users
item.description: Premium Deception campaign uses 250 Android apps to silently sign victims up to paid services

item.title: Mini Shai-Hulud Hits Hundreds of npm Packages in AntV Ecosystem
item.description: Mini Shai-Hulud worm hits Alibaba AntV ecosystem in largest npm supply chain wave to date

item.title: CISA Contractor Exposed Sensitive Credentials in Public GitHub Repository
item.description: <p>CISA is investigating after a contractor’s public GitHub repository exposed AWS GovCloud credentials, internal files, and passwords.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com/article/news-cisa-contractor-github-credential-leak/">CISA Contractor Exposed Sensitive Credentials in Public GitHub Repository</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com">TechRepublic</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Joseph Ofonagoro

item.title: Microsoft Takes Down Malware-Signing Service Behind Ransomware Attacks
item.description:
Microsoft on Tuesday said it disrupted a malware-signing-as-a-service (MSaaS) operation that weaponized the company's Artifact Signing system to deliver malicious code and conduct ransomware and other attacks, compromising thousands of machines and networks across the world.
The tech giant attributed the activity to a threat actor it calls Fox Tempest, which it said offered the MSaaS scheme
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: On AI Security
item.description: <p>Good <a href="https://berryvilleiml.com/docs/no-security-meter-ai.pdf">report</a>:</p> <blockquote><p><b>Executive Summary:</b> Let&#8217;s say you wanted to make sure that your AI is secure. Can you just maximize the security and privacy benchmark and call it a day? Nope, because benchmarks don&#8217;t actually work for measuring AI capabilities (even when they are NOT emergent systemic properties like security). So let&#8217;s take a step back: how do you measure security in the first place? Good question. Over the last 30 years, security engineering for software evolved from black box penetration testing, through whitebox code analysis and architectural risk analysis to de facto process-driven standards like the Building Security In Maturity Model (BSIMM). Software had a very deep impact on business operations, and it appears that AI is going to have an even deeper impact. Will a software security-like measurement move work for AI? Probably. In the meantime we can make real progress in AI security by cleaning up our WHAT piles and managing risk by identifying and applying good assurance processes. (Spoiler alert: no matter what we do, we still don&#8217;t get a security meter for AI, so we need to be extra vigilant about security.)...</p></blockquote>
item.dc:creator: Bruce Schneier

item.title: How CISOs Should Prep for Agentic-Ready AI BOMs
item.description: Finding ways to document both component and execution attributes for AI bills of materials (AI BOMs).
item.dc:creator: Ericka Chickowski, Contributing Writer

item.title: Webworm Deploys EchoCreep and GraphWorm Backdoors Using Discord and MS Graph API
item.description:
Cybersecurity researchers have flagged fresh activity from a China-aligned threat actor known as Webworm in 2025, deploying custom backdoors that employ Discord and Microsoft Graph API for command-and-control (C2 or C&amp;C) communications.
Webworm, first publicly documented by Broadcom-owned Symantec in September 2022, is assessed to be active since at least 2022, targeting government agencies
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: What is Vulnerability Remediation? | UpGuard
item.description: Move beyond "patch everything." Learn the core methodology behind vulnerability remediation and its best practices to achieve effective risk elimination.

item.title: Shadow MCP Servers: The AI Infrastructure You Can't See | UpGuard
item.description: In 2012, it was Dropbox. In 2026, it’s Shadow MCP. Discover why unvetted AI agents are an invisible threat and how to regain total visibility.

item.title: Agent AI is Coming. Are You Ready?
item.description:
New Industry Data Just Released Suggests Not.
On May 19th, 2026, Orchid Security released the results of our Identity Gap: Snapshot 2026. Among the findings, "identity dark matter" (the unseen, unmanaged elements of identity) now overshadows the visible elements 57% vs. 43%. And it couldn't have occurred at a worse time, with enterprises embracing Agent AI with both arms (and unfortunately, as
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: GitHub Breached — Employee Device Hack Led to Exfiltration of 3,800+ Internal Repos
item.description:
GitHub on Tuesday said it's investigating unauthorized access to its internal repositories after the notorious threat actor known as TeamPCP listed the platform's source code and internal organizations for sale on a cybercrime forum.
"While we currently have no evidence of impact to customer information stored outside of GitHub's internal repositories (such as our customers' enterprises,
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: China-Linked Webworm APT Evolves Tactics, Expands to European Targets
item.description: China-linked Webworm APT expands beyond Asia, targeting European government organizations and refining its cyber espionage tactics, according to ESET research

item.title: GitHub Confirms Breach of Internal Repositories Via Malicious VS Code Extension
item.description: The prolific threat group TeamPCP has claimed a hack into GitHub’s internal repositories

item.title: Typosquatting Is No Longer a User Problem. It's a Supply Chain Problem
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Researchers Warn CypherLoc Scareware Has Targeted Millions of Users
item.description: Barracuda reveals new CypherLoc scareware has featured in nearly three million attacks

item.title: Discord DAVE Encrypts Calls, but Not Text Chats
item.description: <p>Discord’s DAVE protocol now protects eligible voice and video calls, but text chats and Stage channels remain outside end-to-end encryption.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com/article/news-discord-dave-end-to-end-encryption/">Discord DAVE Encrypts Calls, but Not Text Chats</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com">TechRepublic</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: TechRepublic Staff

item.title: Webworm: New burrowing techniques
item.description: ESET researchers describe new tools and techniques that the Webworm APT group recently added to its arsenal

item.title: Verizon DBIR: Vulnerability Exploits Overtake Credentials as Top Access Vector
item.description: Verizon DBIR finds 31% of data breaches began with software flaws last year

item.title: Microsoft Releases Mitigation for YellowKey BitLocker Bypass CVE-2026-45585 Exploit
item.description:
Microsoft on Tuesday released a mitigation for a BitLocker bypass vulnerability named YellowKey following its public disclosure last week.
The zero-day flaw, now tracked as CVE-2026-45585, carries a CVSS score of 6.8. It has been described as a BitLocker security feature bypass.
"Microsoft is aware of a security feature bypass vulnerability in Windows publicly referred to as 'YellowKey,'" the
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Interpol's 'Operation Ramz' Pioneers Cross-Region Collabs in Middle East
item.description: While the numbers are modest, the crackdown on cybercrime involved 13 countries in the MENA region, the largest law enforcement collaboration to date.
item.dc:creator: Robert Lemos

item.title: Grafana GitHub Breach Exposes Source Code via TanStack npm Attack
item.description:
Grafana Labs, on May 19, 2026, said an investigation into its recent breach found no evidence of customer production systems or operations being compromised.
It said the scope of the incident is limited to the Grafana Labs GitHub environment, which includes public and private source code along with internal GitHub repositories.
"After the initial assessment, we found that in addition to source
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: What It'll Take to Make AI BOMs Usable in a Modern Security Program
item.description: Five ways CISOs can prepare for consuming AI bills of materials and influence the direction of how they're generated.
item.dc:creator: Ericka Chickowski, Contributing Writer

item.title: What Will Make AI BOMs Real?
item.description: A brief overview of the forces at play that will get more organizations on board with creating and consuming AI bill of materials (BOM).
item.dc:creator: Ericka Chickowski, Contributing Writer

item.title: Verizon DBIR: Enterprises Face a Dangerous Vulnerability Glut
item.description: Verizon's 2026 Data Breach Investigations Report (DBIR) finds that exploits are now involved in 31% of initial access for breaches, while patching lags too far behind the bad guys.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Culafi

item.title: Windows Zero-Day Barrage Continues After Patch Tuesday
item.description: YellowKey, GreenPlasma, and MiniPlasma add to the growing list of vulnerabilities a security researcher disclosed over the past six weeks.
item.dc:creator: Jai Vijayan

item.title: CISA Exposes Secrets, Credentials in 'Private' Repo
item.description: The agency's GitHub repository, publicly available since November 2025, was ironically named &quot;Private-CISA.&quot;
item.dc:creator: Rob Wright

item.title: Stealer Spoofs Google, Microsoft &amp; Apple, Then Backdoors macOS
item.description: The SHub Reaper stealer, which hides behind fake WeChat and Miro installers, marks a shift from ClickFix social engineering to Apple script-based execution.
item.dc:creator: Elizabeth Montalbano

item.title: Mac Users Face New Malware Threat Spoofing Apple, Google, and Microsoft
item.description: <p>A new SHub Reaper macOS infostealer spoofs prompts from Apple, Google, and Microsoft to steal passwords, crypto data, and business files from Macs.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com/article/news-reaper-shub-malware-mac-users/">Mac Users Face New Malware Threat Spoofing Apple, Google, and Microsoft</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com">TechRepublic</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Joseph Ofonagoro

item.title: Trapdoor Android Ad Fraud Scheme Hit 659 Million Daily Bid Requests Using 455 Apps
item.description:
Cybersecurity researchers have disclosed details of a new ad fraud and malvertising operation dubbed Trapdoor targeting Android device users.
The activity, per HUMAN's Satori Threat Intelligence and Research Team, encompassed 455 malicious Android apps and 183 threat actor-owned command-and-control (C2) domains, turning the infrastructure into a pipeline for multi-stage fraud.
"Users
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Microsoft Takes Down Fox Tempest for Providing Ransomware-Enabling Signing Tool
item.description: Microsoft’s Digital Crimes Unit has taken down the infrastructure of Fox Tempest, a prolific cybercrime-enabling threat group

item.title: DirtyDecrypt PoC Released for Linux Kernel CVE-2026-31635 LPE Vulnerability
item.description: Proof-of-concept (PoC) exploit code has now been released for a recently patched security flaw in the Linux kernel that could allow for local privilege escalation (LPE). Dubbed DirtyDecrypt (aka DirtyCBC), the vulnerability was discovered and reported by the Zellic and V12 security team on May 9, 2026, only to be informed by the maintainers that it was a duplicate of a vulnerability that had
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Looking Back, Looking Forward: Digesting a Dynamic Bouillabaisse of Cyber Evolution
item.description: Dark Reading editors reflect on two decades of dramatic change — from perimeter defense to assume-breach strategies — and warn that while AI, cloud, and COVID-19 have transformed the threat landscape, organizations are still failing at fundamental security hygiene that could stop sophisticated attacks in their tracks.
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading Editorial Team

item.title: AdvancedHEALTH Ransomware Claim Includes 2.3M Patient Data Lines
item.description: <p>DragonForce claims it stole 390GB from AdvancedHEALTH, including patient data and minors’ records, as breach notices and legal scrutiny begin.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com/article/news-advancedhealth-ransomware-patient-data-claim/">AdvancedHEALTH Ransomware Claim Includes 2.3M Patient Data Lines</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com">TechRepublic</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Liz Ticong

item.title: AI Raises the Bar on Vulnerability Awareness and Secure-by-Design Software
item.description: AI-powered vulnerability scanning leaves no excuse for unpatched bugs as the EU Cyber Resilience Act pushes firms toward secure-by-design software
